response: Robert M. Fomon was the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co. from 1970 to 1987, and he was also a governor of the New York Stock Exchange. He served as Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. The New York Stock Exchange, located at 11 Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, New York City, was part of the organizations he was associated with, but he did not own or operate the building directly.
